dialogue: Just before sunset, you spot a column of smoke rising beyond a line of trees.
It's too controlled to be a wildfire.
Someone built that fire on purpose.

choices.lockedText: You can't find a good vantage point.

dialogue: From a ridge overlooking the clearing, you count five survivors.
One appears injured.
Another keeps pacing around the fire, frequently checking the woods.

choices.lockedText: You can't make sense of what they're doing.

dialogue: The group begins arguing.
The injured survivor points toward the road.
The others seem determined to stay put.
Weapons immediately turn toward you.
After a tense moment, nobody fires.
'State your business,' a woman near the fire says.
'We were supposed to meet another group here.'
'They never showed.'
The injured man speaks up. 'Or maybe they did. Maybe somebody scared them off.'

choices.lockedText: They aren't interested in taking orders from you.

dialogue: 'Because someone keeps sneaking off at night,' the injured survivor says.
He points at the woman who first greeted you.
'Ever since then, things keep going wrong.'

choices.lockedText: You don't have enough evidence to judge anyone.

choices.text: Tell them to settle it themselves

dialogue: The woman sighs.
'I leave because my husband was supposed to meet us here.'
'Every night I check the road hoping he's still alive.'
You inspect the edge of camp.
You find footprints leading away and returning each night.
Nothing else.

choices.text: Tell the group there's no evidence of betrayal

dialogue: The argument slowly cools.
Nobody seems fully convinced, but weapons lower.
For tonight at least, the group remains together.
The camp fractures almost immediately.
Half the survivors leave before nightfall.
The fire burns long after the shouting stops.
After a lengthy discussion, the group agrees.
They extinguish the fire and prepare to move.
'Maybe surviving means knowing when to let go,' someone mutters.
You decide this isn't your problem.
As darkness falls behind you, raised voices continue around the fire.
The conversation ends instantly.
You realize you've been outnumbered the entire time.
Nobody is feeling charitable.
You continue down the road.
The source of the smoke remains a mystery.
